verdict: AI displacement risk is moderate, with some tasks becoming automated but human trainers still essential for complex behaviors and relationships.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine cognition
reasoning_steps.body: Animal trainers use routine cognitive tasks such as feeding, cleaning, and monitoring animal behavior. While AI can automate some of these tasks, human trainers are still necessary for complex behaviors and relationships.

reasoning_steps.title: Creative judgment
reasoning_steps.body: Animal trainers use creative judgment to understand animal behavior, develop training plans, and make decisions in high-pressure situations. AI is not yet capable of replicating this level of cognitive ability.

evidence_ledger.contribution: Routine cognition tasks are a key part of animal training
Animal trainers have moderate AI displacement risk due to routine cognitive tasks

adjacent_careers.name: Wildlife Conservationist
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both animal trainers and wildlife conservationists work with animals, but conservationists focus on larger-scale ecosystem management

adjacent_careers.name: Veterinarian
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Veterinarians and animal trainers share some skills, but veterinarians focus on medical care and animal health

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered animal training tool released
recent_developments.impact: The new tool automates some routine tasks, but human trainers are still essential for complex behaviors and relationships.

recent_developments.headline: Anthropic Economic Index maps real AI usage across the workforce
recent_developments.impact: Use is concentrated in writing, coding, and analysis — physical and interpersonal work see far less AI involvement.
